rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical thiocyanation/cyclization cascade of N-(2-alkenylaryl)amides with NH4SCN to give 4-thiocyanatomethyl-4H-benzo[d][1,3]oxazines
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of thiocyanate anion to thiocyanate radical (via (SCN)2), which adds to the alkene; the resulting C-radical is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: C-centered benzylic radical from SCN• addition to alkene (intermediate I)
benzylic carbocation after SET oxidation (intermediate II)
protonated oxazine intermediate after intramolecular O-attack (intermediate III)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes SCN- to SCN• (through (SCN)2); SCN• adds to the alkene of 1a to give C-radical I, which is further oxidized at the anode (SET) to carbocation II; intramolecular nucleophilic attack of the amide oxygen and deprotonation deliver oxazine 3a, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3a formed, indicating a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene
probe_reagents.observation: SCN-DPE adduct 3a' detected by GC-MS, confirming SCN radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a and NH4SCN with/without H2SO4
probe_reagents.observation: Eox(2) = 1.48 V vs Eox(1a) = 2.27 V; SCN- is preferentially oxidized; with acid, secondary oxidation of SCN species (to (SCN)3-/(SCN)2-) is observed

notes: Acid (H2SO4) is essential — without it CV shows no 1a oxidation and yield drops sharply; acid promotes secondary oxidation of SCN species, analogous to halide (SCN-/(SCN)3-/(SCN)2-) behavior. Mechanism is the only one proposed; no DFT or KIE reported.
